subject: Physical Education
chapter: Physical Education and Sports for CWSN

cases.passage: The Paralympic Games are an international multi-sport event where athletes with physical disabilities compete. The International Paralympic Committee (IPC) is responsible for organizing the Games. The IPC has introduced several initiatives to promote inclusion and accessibility in sports, such as the IPC Athlete Classification Code. This code ensures that athletes with different disabilities compete fairly and safely. For instance, athletes with visual impairments are classified based on their visual acuity, while athletes with physical impairments are classified based on their functional abilities. The classification system allows athletes to compete against others with similar abilities, promoting fair competition and sportsmanship.

questions.q: What is the primary objective of the IPC Athlete Classification Code?
opts: (A) To promote sportsmanship among athletes
(B) To ensure fair competition among athletes with different disabilities
(C) To provide equal opportunities for athletes with disabilities
(D) To increase the number of participants in the Paralympic Games
questions.explanation: The IPC Athlete Classification Code ensures that athletes with different disabilities compete fairly and safely by classifying them based on their abilities.

questions.q: Which organization is responsible for organizing the Paralympic Games?
opts: (A) International Olympic Committee (IOC)
(B) International Paralympic Committee (IPC)
(C) World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA)
(D) World Health Organization (WHO)
questions.explanation: The International Paralympic Committee (IPC) is responsible for organizing the Paralympic Games.

questions.q: How are athletes with visual impairments classified?
opts: (A) Based on their functional abilities
(B) Based on their visual acuity
(C) Based on their age
(D) Based on their height
questions.explanation: Athletes with visual impairments are classified based on their visual acuity.

questions.q: What is the main advantage of the classification system in the Paralympic Games?
opts: (A) It promotes equal opportunities for athletes with disabilities
(B) It ensures fair competition among athletes with different disabilities
(C) It increases the number of participants in the Games
(D) It reduces the number of athletes with disabilities
questions.explanation: The classification system allows athletes to compete against others with similar abilities, promoting fair competition and sportsmanship.

questions.q: What is the role of the IPC in promoting inclusion and accessibility in sports?
opts: (A) To organize the Paralympic Games
(B) To introduce initiatives to promote inclusion and accessibility
(C) To provide equal opportunities for athletes with disabilities
(D) To increase the number of participants in the Games
questions.explanation: The IPC has introduced several initiatives to promote inclusion and accessibility in sports, such as the IPC Athlete Classification Code.

cases.passage: The National Centre for Promotion of Employment for Disabled People (NCPEDP) is a non-governmental organization that works towards promoting employment opportunities for persons with disabilities. The organization has implemented several initiatives to promote accessibility and inclusion in the workplace, such as the 'Make a Difference (MAD) Awards'. The MAD Awards recognize and reward organizations that have made significant contributions to promoting accessibility and inclusion in the workplace. One such organization is the Indian Railways, which has implemented several accessibility measures, such as ramps and elevators, to make its stations and trains accessible to persons with disabilities.

questions.q: What is the primary objective of the 'Make a Difference (MAD) Awards'?
opts: (A) To recognize and reward organizations that have made significant contributions to promoting accessibility and inclusion in the workplace
(B) To promote employment opportunities for persons with disabilities
(C) To increase the number of organizations that implement accessibility measures
(D) To reduce the number of persons with disabilities in the workplace
questions.explanation: The 'Make a Difference (MAD) Awards' recognize and reward organizations that have made significant contributions to promoting accessibility and inclusion in the workplace.

questions.q: Which organization has implemented several accessibility measures to make its stations and trains accessible to persons with disabilities?
opts: (A) National Centre for Promotion of Employment for Disabled People (NCPEDP)
(B) Indian Railways
(C) International Paralympic Committee (IPC)
(D) World Health Organization (WHO)
questions.explanation: The Indian Railways has implemented several accessibility measures, such as ramps and elevators, to make its stations and trains accessible to persons with disabilities.

questions.q: What is the role of the National Centre for Promotion of Employment for Disabled People (NCPEDP) in promoting employment opportunities for persons with disabilities?
opts: (A) To promote employment opportunities for persons with disabilities
(B) To implement accessibility measures in the workplace
(C) To recognize and reward organizations that have made significant contributions to promoting accessibility and inclusion in the workplace
(D) To reduce the number of persons with disabilities in the workplace
questions.explanation: The National Centre for Promotion of Employment for Disabled People (NCPEDP) works towards promoting employment opportunities for persons with disabilities.

questions.q: What is the main advantage of the accessibility measures implemented by the Indian Railways?
opts: (A) It promotes equal opportunities for persons with disabilities
(B) It ensures fair competition among persons with disabilities
(C) It increases the number of persons with disabilities in the workplace
(D) It makes the workplace accessible to persons with disabilities
questions.explanation: The accessibility measures implemented by the Indian Railways make its stations and trains accessible to persons with disabilities.

questions.q: What is the significance of the 'Make a Difference (MAD) Awards'?
opts: (A) It promotes employment opportunities for persons with disabilities
(B) It recognizes and rewards organizations that have made significant contributions to promoting accessibility and inclusion in the workplace
(C) It increases the number of organizations that implement accessibility measures
(D) It reduces the number of persons with disabilities in the workplace
questions.explanation: The 'Make a Difference (MAD) Awards' recognize and reward organizations that have made significant contributions to promoting accessibility and inclusion in the workplace.
